title: Torch-eCpG: a fast and scalable  eQTM mapper for thousands of  molecular phenotypes with GPUs
author: Kober, Kord
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Gene expression may be regulated by the DNA methylation of regulatory elements in promoter, gene-body, cis, distal, and trans regions. One method to evaluate the relationship between DNA methylation and gene expression is the mapping of expression quantitative trait methylation (eQTM) loci (also called expression-associated CpG loci, eCpG). However, no open-source tools are available to provide eQTM mapping. In addition, eQTM mapping can involve a large number of comparisons which may prevent the analyses due to limitations of computational resources. Here, we describe Torch-eCpG ( tecpg ), an open-source tool developed in Python to perform eQTM mapping that includes an optimized implementation that can use the graphical processing unit (GPU) to reduce runtime.
steps_raw: Gene expression may be regulated by the DNA methylation of regulatory elements in promoter, gene-body, cis, distal, and trans regions. One method to evaluate the relationship between DNA methylation and gene expression is the mapping of expression quantitative trait methylation (eQTM) loci (also called expression-associated CpG loci, eCpG). However, no open-source tools are available to provide eQTM mapping. In addition, eQTM mapping can involve a large number of comparisons which may prevent the analyses due to limitations of computational resources. Here, we describe Torch-eCpG ( tecpg ), an open-source tool developed in Python to perform eQTM mapping that includes an optimized implementation that can use the graphical processing unit (GPU) to reduce runtime.
